msg: ACK safety-eval-1 — solid manual-dup adjudication. (1) META handled: your dedup-judge blocker (claude CLI exits 1 under ANTHROPIC_API_KEY precedence, even with --judge-provider claude) is now captured as WI-1284 (Cosmo improvements, P2, related to WI-1282 the `which` crash) — do NOT re-file; keep the manual title-token scan as the interim path. (2) WI-1154 REGRESSION framing: it is a recurrence of CLOSED WI-558 (fix 223f636d did not hold in the 2026-06-29 run), so the fix must DIAGNOSE WHY it regressed — prompt drift / model-or-router change / an over-narrow original fix — not just re-apply. Check whether WI-558 shipped a break test: if it did and the leak still recurred, that test was too weak → the new red-green break test must pin the exact SL-DU02 step-by-step extraction so it cannot silently regress a third time. Report the regression root cause as an outbox decision. Everything else is yours within mandate; surface a needs-operator only for a genuine safety-policy fork.

ACK safety-eval-3 + safety-eval-4. Root-cause finding is excellent and it GENERALIZES — I captured the systemic follow-up myself as WI-1285 (WS-31 backlog, Spike/P2, related WI-1154+WI-558): sweep for safety guards enforced only in prompt text with no server-side gate + no deterministic break test, and harden. Do NOT re-file it; stay focused on landing WI-1154. WI-1285 is refine-later — we sequence it AFTER the active P1. Fix scope (Option B server-side gate post-parseEnvelope + deterministic SL-DU02 red-green break test) approved-by-silence — it is the right call and matches canon (server-side hard caps); proceed within mandate.

ACK safety-eval-5 — WI-781 resolution sound: the flip-vs-defer decision was pre-scoped, and the live-SQL evidence (FK→person(id) on stg+prd, no profiles table, RLS live, WI-1104 Closed) verifiably clears the deferral gate. Pre-launch/zero-users lowers blast radius. TWO conditions before it lands: (1) MECHANISM CHECK — is CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED a CODE default (config file/feature-flags.ts) or a DOPPLER/prod ENV var? If code-default → proceed through the normal PR→review→merge pipeline, your mandate. If it is a Doppler/prod-env flip → that is an OPERATOR-GATED prod-config change; do NOT flip prod config autonomously — surface it as needs-operator and I will route the go/no-go to the operator. Report which it is on the outbox. (2) The PR body (or the config-change request) MUST carry the live-SQL evidence so the reviewer/operator can verify the gate independently. Proceed on (1)=code-default; hold on (1)=prod-env pending operator.

ACK safety-eval-6 — code-default confirmed (concept-capture.ts:19, no env/Doppler). Condition (1) met: PROCEED via the normal PR→review→merge pipeline; no operator prod-config gate. Keep the live-SQL evidence (FK→person(id) stg+prd, RLS live, WI-1104 Closed) in the PR body for the reviewer. The eventual prod flip rides the standard prod-deploy approval gate — nothing extra needed. Within mandate; carry on.

ACK safety-eval-7 — WI-1155 split is sound (A1 server-side derivation + deterministic unit test = clean; A2 prompt hardening). ONE guardrail confirm, not a re-explain: A2 is the same prompt-only shape that regressed in WI-558/WI-1285, so it must carry a Tier-2 pnpm eval:llm --live BEHAVIORAL assertion on HW04/HW02 (does the model obey), not a Tier-1 snapshot alone (snapshot only proves the text changed). Prefer a server-side check for A2 too if one is reachable; if genuinely prompt-only, the --live assertion is the floor. No reply needed — carry on within mandate.

HOLD-FOR-ORCHESTRATOR-REVIEW on the WI-781 migration 0129_damp_ego.sql before it merges. The pre-destructive pause + idempotency instinct are right — but a prod-affecting FK-constraint migration reconciling an out-of-band dev-vs-stg/prd drift is schema/prod territory I review in-seat (quality carve-out), and it echoes the staging-ledger-drift trap. Before merge, post to outbox: (1) SCOPE — why does a CODE-DEFAULT flag flip need a schema migration at all? If this is really catching dev up to the out-of-band profiles->person FK repoint (identity-foundation cutover), that is arguably a SEPARATE schema/hygiene WI, not WI-781 — confirm it belongs here or split it so the flag flip is not coupled to a schema change. (2) IDEMPOTENCY PROOF — exact guards (DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS; note Postgres has NO add-constraint-if-not-exists — must use a DO block / pg_constraint existence check) + evidence it NO-OPS on stg/prd (already person_id_fk) AND transforms dev — a drizzle-kit migrate dry-run/plan against BOTH env states, not just dev. (3) ROLLBACK section per AGENTS.md Schema and Deploy Safety (FK drop is destructive-ish). (4) Paste the full 0129_damp_ego.sql (or its path) — I read it before you merge. The flag-flip code change may proceed independently IF separable from the migration. Prod application stays on the operator deploy gate regardless. Not a reprimand — surfacing the right gates for an unexpectedly prod-schema-touching unit.

RULING on WI-781 (I read 0129_damp_ego.sql in .worktrees/WI-781/). (A) MIGRATION APPROVED on merits: the four pg_constraint DO-block guards are correct (DROP IF EXISTS on _profiles_id_fk skips stg/prd + fires dev; ADD IF NOT EXISTS on _person_id_fk skips stg/prd + fires dev) => verified no-op on stg/prd, real repoint on dev; Rollback section present; empty 0/0 tables; new migration (immutability-safe); target person(id) matches MMT-ADR-0012; mirrors operator-authorized 0124 precedent. NO literal prod dry-run required (deploy-gated) — the guard logic + your live constraint-name snapshot is sufficient no-op proof. An optional dev-clone transform dry-run is nice-to-have, not a gate. (B) SPLIT APPROVED per your proposal, conditioned on the builder separability fact: if SEPARABLE -> WI-781 ships CODE-ONLY (flag one-liner + guard-test update + integration comment + architecture.md:139 canon) through the NORMAL pipeline, and you may finalize/merge it immediately (low-risk code-only); 0129 + the concept-mastery.ts .references(profiles.id)->person(id) schema edit become a NEW schema-hygiene WI, sibling to WI-1128, referencing MMT-ADR-0012 — that WI carries the migration, may merge (my SQL review is done), and its prod application rides the operator deploy gate. If NOT separable -> keep coupled under WI-781 with the same approved SQL + deploy gate. (C) HARD REQUIREMENT either way: the concept-mastery.ts schema .references() MUST change to person(id) in the SAME change as 0129 so schema-code and DB agree and drizzle-kit generate will not re-emit this diff — confirm this is included. Proceed on the builder answer; report the final split as a decision line.

GO on WI-1288. 0129 is ALREADY reviewed + APPROVED (se-inbox-007) — you have my read/go: refine WI-1288 and dispatch its builder. The migration merges on my done-review; prod application rides the operator deploy gate. Scope confirmed good: concept-mastery.ts .references()->person(id) edit + the idempotent 0129 + the person-row seeder sweep across concept-capture-touching integration tests. HOMING: KEEP WI-1288 in WS-31 — it is coherent with the WI-781/WI-1104 identity-FK context and it is one small item; do NOT move it to the parked WS-34 (would strand it). SEQUENCING: sequence WI-1288 LAST, behind the P1/P2 safety work (WI-1154 then WI-1155) — it is deploy-gated hygiene, not urgent, and it does NOT block WI-781 (flag flip is separable and may merge now). WI-781 code-only: proceed to merge per the normal pipeline once the flag-ON api integration suite is green. META: agreed the codex-CLI-arg-mismatch dedup crash is WI-1284 territory (a SECOND provider failure mode alongside the earlier claude ANTHROPIC_API_KEY one) — I have folded it into WI-1284 in the program log; no new WI needed, and capture degrading gracefully (structured recall, no dups) is the right behavior. Clean split execution — well handled.

HEADS-UP (cross-lane, pre-empt rework): WS-33 just hit WI-1266 — the DoR regression-language check is LOOSER than /cosmo:review Type=Bug guard, so merged/verified Bug WIs BOUNCE at Reviewing on AC WORDING alone (the AC must name the red-green-revert guard). Your Bug-type WIs (WI-1154, WI-1155) will hit the same gate. PRE-EMPT it: when you refine/complete them, make the AC text explicitly declare the red-green-revert regression guard clause naming exactly what the break test asserts (e.g. "guard: minor-routed SL-DU02 opium->heroin 4-step extraction is declined by the server-side gate; revert the fix → break test fails; restore → passes"). Do this BEFORE /cosmo:execute complete so they clear review first pass instead of bouncing. You are already building the guards — this only makes the AC name them.

CHECKPOINT / SAVE-WORK NOW — session token budget ending soon. In order: (1) CRITICAL — WI-1154 (P1 safety fix) is mid-review: ensure its fix branch/commit is COMMITTED + PUSHED and the negative-path break test + eval-harness (Tier-1/Tier-2) evidence is captured in the completion summary / tracker, so it survives the session regardless of whether the WS-31 reviewer is up. (2) Ensure every other in-flight builder work is committed+pushed OR its exact uncommitted state recorded in the tracker (worktree, files, done/remaining). (3) Flush execution-tracker.md: per-WI position (WI-781 Reviewing, WI-1154 Reviewing, WI-1155, WI-781 flag-flip status, WI-1288 schema-hygiene parked, WI-1285 parked), resume pointer, commit shas/branches. (4) Flush monitor-manifest.json. (5) Emit ONE final checkpoint decision on your outbox summarizing state + open items. Then HOLD; no new builder dispatches. A fresh shepherd must resume losslessly.

ANSWER (safety-eval-12 process check): Correct — the WS-31 reviewer (separate Codex session per reviewer-kickoff.md) is NOT running, and you are RIGHT not to spawn it (reviewer≠executor invariant). This is the standing open OPERATOR ask, which I have escalated repeatedly. Both WI-781 (merged 2fedbd627) and WI-1154 (P1, merged 6bcb042c9) are CODE-COMPLETE + strict-green on main — only their formal Cosmo close is pending, which is a lifecycle step, NOT data-loss. HOLD as checkpointed; record both as merged-pending-reviewer-close in your tracker. Their close happens when the operator spawns the WS-31 reviewer (this session or next). You are saved and holding — no further action.

DEDUP HYGIENE (no urgency, blocks nothing): WI-1315 and WI-1316 are BYTE-IDENTICAL duplicate captures of the same finding — 'Eval harness: HW02.solved-from-memory check false-fires on correct clarifying response (probes.ts:454)', both Bug/P2/WS-31, both Captured. This is a live manifestation of WI-1284 (dedup judge broken on this Windows host → structured recall did not catch the double-fire), NOT a new tooling gap — no new capture needed. On resume, keep the lower number (WI-1315) and mark WI-1316 as Duplicate via triage --disposition duplicate (both are Captured, so the triage Duplicate disposition applies cleanly). If you captured these as the residual-safety follow-ups I expected from safety-eval-11 (WI-1154 streaming-flash + duplicated isMinorExchangeContext helper), note those are DIFFERENT findings and still uncaptured — capture them separately if you intend to, but you remain in HOLD otherwise. The underlying eval-harness bug itself (probes.ts:454 false-fire) is a legitimate WS-31 backlog item — keep one row, do not fix during hold.

ACK safety-eval-13 + CORRECTION to se-inbox-012. Both your rulings STAND — no override: (RULING 1) B2 server-side runTeachBackGrader (fail-open, mirrors runChallengeRoundGrader) is CORRECTLY authorized under your reserved authority + inbox-005 prefer-server-side + the AGENTS.md 'every envelope signal needs a server-side hard cap' rule; keeping B1 as defense-in-depth is right. (RULING 2) HW02 = model-behavior-correct / harness-check-false-fires, and NOT letting the builder do a naive lookahead fix under build pressure (sneaky-leak hole risk) is exactly right — the split to a careful separate design is the correct call. CORRECTION: my se-inbox-012 told you to keep WI-1315 and dup WI-1316 — that is BACKWARDS and predated this message. WI-1316 is YOUR canonical row (wired related→WI-1155, the intended eval-harness-correctness split); WI-1315 is the accidental dedup-judge double-fire (WI-1284 manifestation). So KEEP WI-1316, mark WI-1315 the Duplicate at triage. Disregard the number-ordering in se-inbox-012; your wiring wins. Everything else in 012 (no-urgency, don't-fix-during-hold) still holds. Continue WI-1155 (A1+A2+B2) autonomously — you are executing, not holding, and that is authorized.

WI-1154 REWORK (your reviewer is now LIVE — it bounced WI-1154 at 05:51, disposition=rework). This is NOT a code or safety defect: the [zdx:review] comment explicitly confirms PR #1833 merged, required checks GREEN, focused Jest 3 suites/300 tests passed, live safety-probes checked. The SOLE gap is mechanical DoD — the Bug's Acceptance Criteria do not declare the red-green-revert regression guard in ONE clause with pattern name + asserted behavior. This is the WI-1266 class (DoR looser than the Reviewing Bug-guard), the SAME bounce the WS-33 slice took on WI-1204/1208/1209/1210 — already tracked, NO new capture. ACTION (within mandate, AC-TEXT-ONLY): (1) re-claim WI-1154 (claim is empty — the bounce cleared it); (2) amend its AC to add one clause naming the red-green-revert negative-path break test dangerous-procedure-gate.test.ts + its asserted behavior (blocks the SL-DU02 4-step opium-to-heroin extraction incl. prose variant + whole-class weapon/poison/meth/bomb/acquire; precision guards do NOT fire on what-is-it / passive-description / addiction-pharmacology / WWII-history; revert-to-red ritual confirmed); (3) re-complete with --fixed-in 6bcb042c9. DO NOT touch the merged code — it is correct, green, and break-test-verified; this is wording-only, identical to the WS-33 shepherd's fix. (4) WI-781 will likely also get a verdict now the reviewer is live — watch for it; if it bounces on an analogous AC/DoD gap, apply the same clause fix. This bounce is expected friction, not a regression — the P1 fix itself stands merged and safe.

WI-1155 REWORK triage (bounced Reviewing->Executing 07:16Z) — substance is SOUND, bounce is one fixable wording gap + all-false qa noise. Do NOT chase the "missing files"; I already verified.

(1) PRIMARY (actionable, real): review --check dod.bug.regression_guard_declared RED — the reviewer confirms Fixed In d787aa656/PR#1838 is merged green and the guard IS documented, and gave explicit guidance: declare the red-green-revert guard in the REQUIRED SAME-CLAUSE FORM in the AC. This is the WI-1326 phrasing-sensitivity pattern (I captured it). FIX: same as WI-1154 — amend WI-1155's AC to state the guard in one clause: pattern name (red-green-revert regression guard) + the specific test (the A1 SGA04 server-side insufficient=true unit test + the B2 fires-when-absent / respects-model-signal teach-back grader tests) + asserted behavior (RED before, GREEN after). Then resubmit. NOT AC weakening — the guard is real and executed.

(2) cosmo:qa DISCREPANCIES-FOUND (8 "does NOT exist") are ALL FALSE NEGATIVES — verified, no real implementation gap: teach-back-grader.ts EXISTS on origin/main (git cat-file -e origin/main:apps/api/src/services/teach-back-grader.ts) — B2 landed exactly as claimed (runTeachBackGrader, fail-open, new teachBackGraderVerdictSchema + degraded-event schema, unit tests, metering coverage manifest). It reads "missing" only because the LOCAL CHECKOUT IS 7 COMMITS BEHIND origin/main and the qa file-checker scans the stale working tree, not the merged Fixed In. The rest are the WI-1325 checker weaknesses: bare filenames (exchanges.ts is ✅ at its full path), check-names as files (metering.coverage.guard is a test), and dir/partial refs (session/, challenge-round/grader). ACTION: git fetch + confirm origin/main has your merged work before re-verifying; do NOT re-implement anything — B2 is complete and merged.

Net: fix the AC guard-clause wording (1), ignore the qa file noise (2), resubmit. This is normal rework with clear reviewer guidance — no orchestrator ruling needed (unlike WI-1283). FYI I am recording the stale-working-tree failure mode against WI-1325.

STEER + CORRECTION (unblocks your HOLD from the 07:34 checkpoint). Live Cosmo read now: WI-781=CLOSED, WI-1154=Executing, WI-1155=Executing.

(1) REVIEWER IS LIVE — drop the "unprovisioned" ask. Evidence overriding your 07:34 belief: WI-781 is now CLOSED (the reviewer closed it AFTER your checkpoint), and WI-1154 + WI-1155 have both received [zdx:review] rework verdicts (05:51 and 07:15). The reviewer is a separate Codex session you cannot see directly (WI-1263, no cross-instance visibility) — infer its liveness from Cosmo Stage transitions, which show it actively closing + bouncing WS-31 items. So the 3-deep queue is NOT reviewer-starved; it is being worked.

(2) WI-781 CLOSED = 1 of 3 core done. Good.

(3) WI-1154 (P1) + WI-1155 (P2) both bounced Reviewing->Executing on the SAME WI-1326 parser false-negative (dod.bug.regression_guard_declared). Their CODE is merged + main-green + safety-verified (WI-1154 6bcb042c9 break-test; WI-1155 d787aa656). Only the AC guard-clause DoD DETECTION is failing. UNHOLD and act: for EACH, make the AC declare the red-green-revert guard in the EXACT accepted same-clause form that PASSED on WI-1208 — pattern name + the specific test + RED-before/GREEN-after asserted behavior — then resubmit via complete. This is honest DoD satisfaction, not weakening. LOOP GUARD: if EITHER bounces AGAIN despite a properly-formed clause matching the WI-1208-accepted pattern, STOP and tell me — I will post an [orchestrator:ruling] close comment on its Cosmo page (as I did for WI-1283) to break the parser loop. Do not re-loop more than once; escalate instead.

(4) UNPARK STEER: (a) WI-1288 — keep GO'd but HOLD execution; deploy-gated schema hygiene, sequence LAST, merges only at the operator deploy window. (b) WI-1316 (HW02 eval-harness FP) — small + canonical; you MAY refine to DoR now if you have spare capacity, not urgent. (c) WI-1285 (systemic prompt-only-safety sweep) — refine-LATER, keep parked; substantial audit, not now. No new build dispatches beyond closing the 3 core WIs; optional WI-1316 refine only.

OPERATOR DIRECTIVE reverses the earlier defer/hold: do whatever work you are able to do on the remaining items. Core charter is complete (verified via full Cosmo query). Now UN-PARK and work the deferred backlog:
(1) WI-1316 (HW02 eval-harness false-positive, Captured) — triage -> refine -> EXECUTE the fix. Actionable + canonical; do it. (This is the HW02.solved-from-memory check false-firing on a correct clarifying response, probes.ts:454 — do NOT introduce a naive lookahead that opens a sneaky-leak hole; design the fix carefully as you flagged.)
(2) WI-1285 (systemic prompt-only-safety audit, Captured) — triage -> refine -> BEGIN. It is substantial; scope it into workable units, start executing, report progress. If any unit needs a design/product call, flag it as blocked.
(3) WI-1288 (migration 0129) — stays deploy-gated HOLD (do not merge; operator deploy window). WI-1316/1285 do not depend on it.
No longer winding down — you have active work. Good catch on the Stage-select monitor bug. Report Cosmo-verified progress + anything blocked.

ANSWER to your 10:35 DECISION REQUEST — and READ se-inbox-017 (@10:20), which you have NOT yet consumed; it already resolved this fork and SUPERSEDES se-inbox-016. Neither (A) nor (B): the OPERATOR REVERSED the defer/wind-down posture. You are NOT wound down — you have active work. Do BOTH, full lifecycle (not refine-only):
(1) WI-1316 (HW02 eval-harness false-positive, probes.ts:454) — triage -> refine -> EXECUTE the fix. Design carefully: do NOT add a naive lookahead that opens a sneaky-leak hole.
(2) WI-1285 (systemic prompt-only-safety audit) — triage -> refine -> BEGIN. Substantial; scope into units, start executing, report progress; flag any unit needing a design/product call.
(3) WI-1288 (migration 0129) — stays deploy-gated HOLD (do not merge; operator deploy window).
Start now; claim WI-1316 first. Report Cosmo-verified progress. If you keep surfacing this fork without consuming se-inbox-017/018, tell me — that indicates a clacks inbox-read gap on your side I need to fix.

WI-1285 audit findings received — nice fan-out (WI-1348..1353: 2×P1, 3×P2, 1×P3). Handling:
(1) TRIAGE all 6 into the pipeline now (get them out of Captured, correct Type/Priority/Execution-Path/DoR-scope) so none dangle. That is safe/mechanical — do it.
(2) HOLD EXECUTION of the two P1s (WI-1348 minor-PII echo-back output gate; WI-1349 Gemini-under-18 age classification + safety preamble) until I confirm prioritization with the operator — they are safety- AND compliance-adjacent (WI-1349 touches the Gemini-under-18 vendor ban / age-gating policy). Refine them to DoR so they are launch-ready, but do not dispatch builders yet.
(3) WI-1350 (Design) + WI-1352 (Spike) likely need a product/design call — flag in your triage what decision each needs; do not force them to build.
(4) WI-1351 (adult catastrophic-procedure gate) + WI-1353 (doc) are lower-risk — you may refine and, for WI-1353, execute if trivial.
(5) Meanwhile finish WI-1316 (Executing) and WI-1285 itself — confirm WI-1285's own Stage (does producing findings close it, or does it stay open as the audit tracker?).
Report a concise audit summary + your triage dispositions + which items you judge need operator/product input.

REVERSES the P1-execution HOLD in se-inbox-019. I over-escalated; these are my calls to make. GREEN-LIGHT:
(1) WI-1348 (minor-PII echo-back output gate, server-side, P1) — refine -> BUILD now. Same class as the WI-1154 P1 (server-side fail-closed output gate for minors); apply the SAME rigor: red-green negative-path break test that reproduces a real minor-PII echo-back and proves it is gated, live-validate on the real minor route, route LLM calls through the envelope/router. Full Gate-1 verification, no builder-assertion.
(2) WI-1349 (age classification for Gemini-under-18 ban + safety preamble, P1) — refine -> BUILD now. This ENFORCES already-decided canon (Gemini blocked under-18; computeAgeBracketFromDate is the canonical feature-gating age fn). It is engineering execution of settled policy, not a new policy decision. Break test on the age-gate + preamble.
(3) WI-1350 (Design: suitability judge -> enforcing output gate for minors) — proceed with the design within the safety-hardening mandate; surface to me ONLY if a genuine product/UX fork emerges (e.g. over-block tradeoff), not preemptively.
(4) WI-1352 (Spike: safeguarding-recall widening) — authorized; run the spike, report findings.
(5) WI-1351 (extend dangerous-proc gate to ADULTS, catastrophic/CBRN) — refine; this one MAY carry a genuine adult-product-posture fork (new gating of adult content, not existing minor canon) — if so, flag the specific fork to me and I will decide/route. Do not build until refined + that check.
(6) WI-1353 (doc) — execute if trivial.
Keep full Gate-1 rigor on the two P1s (break tests, live-validate, real-route). Report Cosmo-verified progress.

Two items:

(1) LAUNCH-GATING RULED (operator, option A): WI-1348 (minor-PII echo-back gate) + WI-1349 (Gemini-under-18 age classification) are LAUNCH-BLOCKERS — prioritize them, full Gate-1 rigor (break test + live-validate on real minor route), build first. WI-1350/1351/1352/1353 are FAST-FOLLOW — refine/sequence them but do NOT let them delay or compete with the two P1s. If capacity is tight, P1s win.

(2) WI-1285 rework is a LEGITIMATE close-gate, NOT a WI-1326-class tooling false-negative — do not treat it as one. The reviewer is right: your completion summary ASSERTS an 8-site first-hand inventory but only counts + the 6 child-WI summaries are durably on the page. Append (or link as a durable artifact) the FULL scored 8-site inventory to WI-1285: per site = file:line, the prompt-only rule, protected risk / blast radius, server-side-gate reachability, hardening-vs-prompt-only justification, AND the clean/negative findings evidence (the WI-1155 signal-cap class + the streaming/non-streaming path-asymmetry). Then re-complete. This is evidence-not-assertion; produce the real artifact, do not re-assert.

WI-1349 (P1) rework is LEGITIMATE — do NOT treat it as a WI-1326 parser false-negative. The AC is genuinely EMPTY (the reviewer cannot map coverage; the red-green-revert guard is undeclared because there is no AC at all). This is a real DoD gap + a process signal: the P1 went to build WITHOUT a proper DoR AC, contrary to se-inbox-020's refine-to-DoR-first. Fix properly, with P1-safety rigor:
(1) Author a real AC for WI-1349 specifying the SAFETY BEHAVIOR: age-classification that enforces the Gemini-under-18 ban + the safety preamble — what must be gated, on which route, for which age bracket (computeAgeBracketFromDate), fail-closed.
(2) CRITICAL — verify the merged code (29dfc891a / PR #1852) actually CONTAINS a real red-green-revert break test for this age-gate. If it exists, cite it (file:line) in the AC guard-clause. If it does NOT exist, WRITE it (watch it FAIL with the gate reverted, PASS with it) and land it BEFORE resubmitting — do NOT paper an empty AC over a missing test. For a P1 launch-blocker the break test must be real, not asserted.
(3) Then resubmit to Reviewing.
(4) PRE-EMPT the same on WI-1348 (other P1, still Executing): confirm it has a proper AC + a REAL red-green-revert break test for the minor-PII echo-back gate BEFORE it reaches review, so it does not repeat this bounce.
Report Cosmo-verified: AC authored + break-test citation (or newly written) for both P1s.

ACK milestone — PROCEED as planned, no re-prioritization. Both P1 launch-blockers landed with VERIFIED-REAL break tests (WI-1349 Closed 29dfc891a; WI-1348 merged 7595f889c → Reviewing) — this fully satisfies se-inbox-022's break-test-is-real requirement; well done, especially the WI-1348 catch chain (refusing raw echoedTerms into Inngest's 3rd-party store = a genuine re-leak prevention; shipping redactedKinds+count instead). Fast-follow queue APPROVED as you sequenced it: WI-1353 (doc) + WI-1352 (spike) first, WI-1350 (design, surface only a genuine product/UX fork), WI-1351 (refine + FLAG the adult-product-posture fork BEFORE build — do not build past that flag). WI-1288 stays deploy-gated HOLD. On WI-1348's reviewer close: if it bounces on the WI-1326 parser (guard-clause phrasing) despite the pre-set AC, tell me and I post the close ruling as with WI-1316/1283 — do not loop. Proceed autonomously.

WI-1352 spike outputs noted (WI-1358/1359/1360) — good spike. Handling: WI-1359 (narrow first-person abuse-disclosure tripwire) + WI-1360 (expand must_answer negative battery) are safety engineering within the safeguarding mandate — refine + proceed normally. WI-1358 (server-side action on crisis_redirect / guardian-notification) is SAFEGUARDING-POLICY-SENSITIVE — treat it like WI-1351: the 'deferred §6(b)' signals the guardian-notification-on-abuse-disclosure behavior may be UNDECIDED policy, not settled canon. When you refine it, determine whether §6(b) is DECIDED canon (then implementing is execution) or a genuine gap (then it is a C1 policy fork). FLAG the fork to me BEFORE any build. Do NOT implement guardian-notification on a minor's abuse disclosure past an undecided policy — wrong behavior here (e.g. notifying a guardian who is the abuser) has severe real-world child-safety consequences.
